display_name: Joseph Howell and Embry Howell (joint interview)
role_summary: Joint oral history of Joseph Howell and Embry Howell; the lowest single-entry neighbor counts in the audited joint-interview subset (peak 42), suggesting a short or topically focused transcript.
ai_reading: The Howells' top semantic neighbors are Junius Williams (entry 72, count 42, Newark Black Power Conference organizer), Joan Trumpauer Mulholland (entry 60, Freedom Rider), Martha Prescod Norman Noonan (entry 83, SNCC field organizer), C. T. Vivian (entry 134, SCLC), and Kathleen Cleaver (entry 73, BPP Communications Secretary). The cluster crosses Northern Black Power, Southern direct-action, SNCC fieldwork, and the BPP, an unusually broad spread for a low-count interview. Their position on the Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense axis is +0.84, far toward Armed Self-Defense, joining the catalog's recurring pattern of joint and family-member testimonies landing on the Self-Defense side. Without external biographical sourcing in this iteration, the catalog notes the cross-cluster pattern as the structural observation: the Howells' transcript reads as a movement-bridge voice spanning factions that did not historically partner.
biographical_paragraph: This is a joint oral history at the Library of Congress as entry 67 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Joint interviews in the corpus combine the testimony of two or more interviewees in a single recording; the catalog page treats the embedding signature of the joint transcript as one unit (see the AI's reading above). Independent biographical sourcing for each individual interviewee is pending; future enrichment may split this entry into separate per-person pages if individual biographical material becomes accessible.
